attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
All 15 tests pass.

## Naming Evaluation (First Priority)

- `quotePremium(items)`: Clear - returns premium for a list of items. Keep.
- `processScenario(scenario)`: Clear. Keep.
- `policyBase`, `itemSurcharges`, `firstInsurance`, `countsByType`: All reveal intent well.
- `BLOCK_OF_3_PRICE`: Clear constant name.
- `BASE_PREMIUM_BY_ITEM_TYPE`: Clear.

All names are descriptive and reveal intent. No renaming needed.

## Current Code Mass Analysis

The current implementation has two loops over `items` — one to count types, and one for surcharges. This is conceptual duplication of iteration. Let me see if simplifying helps without anticipating future tests.

Current mass (approximate breakdown of `quotePremium`):
- Constants: ~6 (0, 0, 0, /10, etc.)
- Bindings: ~11 (countsByType, item, type, count, policyBase, itemSurcharges, firstInsurance, etc.)
- Invocations: ~10 (entries, has, ceil, ?., +=, etc.)
- Conditionals: 3 (if block-eligible, if high-enchant, if cursed)
- Loops: 3 (for-of items, for-of entries, for-of items)
- Assignments: ~6 (countsByType[type]=, policyBase+=, itemSurcharges+=, etc.)

Rough total: ~6 + 11 + 20 + 12 + 15 + 36 = ~100

## Rule 3 Evaluation: Duplication

There is duplication of `BASE_PREMIUM_BY_ITEM_TYPE[item.type]` in the surcharge calculation (lines 38, 42). Both surcharges multiply the same base by a rate. I could extract a helper, but that may not help much.

There is also iteration duplication: two loops over `items`. However, combining them would mix concerns (counts depend on full pass first for block-of-3 logic). Keeping them separate reveals intent better.

## Rule 4 Evaluation: Fewest Elements

The surcharge block could be slightly simplified by computing surcharge rate per item using sum of applicable rates:

Let me try one focused refactoring: factor out the per-item surcharge rate computation, which removes duplication of the `BASE_PREMIUM_BY_ITEM_TYPE[item.type]` lookup and clarifies the additive nature of surcharges.

content.text:
All 15 tests still pass.

## Refactoring Summary

**Naming Evaluation**: All names already reveal intent clearly. No renames.

**Refactoring Applied** (Rule 3 — remove duplication):
- Extracted a per-item `rate` accumulator so `BASE_PREMIUM_BY_ITEM_TYPE[item.type]` is referenced only once per item instead of duplicated across each surcharge branch.
- The shape of the code now makes additive surcharges explicit: each applicable rate adds to one accumulator, then multiplies the base premium once.

**Mass Change** (surcharge loop only, before → after):
- Before: 2 conditionals + 2 multiplications + 2 assignments + 2 lookups of `BASE_PREMIUM_BY_ITEM_TYPE[item.type]` = ~31
- After: 1 binding (rate) + 2 conditionals + 2 assignments + 1 multiplication + 1 lookup + 1 final assignment = ~27
- Modest mass reduction, clearer additive structure.

**Anticipation Check**: This refactoring does NOT enable any not-yet-active `it.todo()` test. The behavior is identical — surcharges are summed exactly as before. The next test (`loyalty discount`) requires customer info that isn't even threaded through `quotePremium` yet; this refactoring does nothing to ease that work prematurely.

**Other potential refactorings considered but rejected**:
- Merging the two passes over `items` (count loop + surcharge loop): would obscure the two distinct concerns (block pricing vs. per-item surcharges). Rule 2 (clarity) trumps Rule 4 (fewer elements).
- Extracting a `surchargeRateFor(item)` helper: adds an element without revealing more intent at the only call site. Defer until a second caller appears.

Refactor Phase Complete:
- Refactoring: extracted per-item surcharge rate accumulator (deduplicated base-premium lookup)
- Tests: 15 passing
- File: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-22_10-41-57_claim-office-example-mapping_v4.2-shared-context_opus-4-7-portkey-no-thinking/src/claim-office.ts
